title: Na'vi Vocabulary
objective: Learn essential Na'vi words for family and the natural world.

data.text: Now that you know the sounds, let's learn some words. Na'vi has a relatively small vocabulary — around 2,600 words — but the words you'll learn here are the foundation everything else is built on.
We'll start with two groups: people in your family, and things in the natural world. The Na'vi live close to nature, and many of their core words reflect that.

data.description: The general word for a person, regardless of age or gender. Tap to hear it.
Mother. The apostrophe is a glottal stop — a tiny pause in your throat, like the gap in the English 'uh-oh'.
Father. Stress falls on the first syllable: SEM-pul.
Child. Notice the leading apostrophe — that's another glottal stop right at the start of the word.
Brother. The 'tsm' cluster is unusual to English speakers — practice it slowly.
Sister. Same 'tsm' cluster — once you've got brother, this one is easy.

data.instruction: Match each Na'vi word with its meaning.
Listen, then tap and hold the microphone to record yourself saying it.

data.description: Tree. Trees are sacred to the Na'vi — Hometree (Kelutral) is the largest one of all.
Fire. The 'tx' is an ejective — pronounced with a small pop of air, harder than a regular 't'.
The mountain banshee — the flying creature Na'vi warriors bond with. A core part of their culture.
The pack-hunting predator of Pandora. Fast, dangerous, and feared even by experienced hunters.

data.instruction: Listen to the word and choose what it means.
Listen to this word, then record yourself.

data.text: 🎉 Great work! You now know your first ten Na'vi words. In the next lesson, you'll learn how to put them to use by introducing yourself.
